transcript: [sfx] Oh, hi! This is Jim callin’. Ah, how ya doin’?

Hey, good! Uh, so yeah, Joe, I got a buncha pot metal from Acapulco and I’m trying to forge a buncha bakeware here in my foundry. And I’m thinkin’ of getting into an alloy of some kind instead. Do you guys, maybe, have any pig iron available that I could purchase? Or…? [delay]—
—I really don’t have—yeah, I really don’t have anything like that—
—Everything we carry is, pretty much, structural.
—so all the shapes and the sheets and the plates and angles and channels and flat bar and that kinda stuff.
Well yeah! That’s what I mean! I’d like to gild the fulcrum. So, maybe could I add some nickel to it? Something along those lines? ‘Cause, ah—

Oh, that’s okay! That’s okay! [delay] Uh, I’m temperin’ my scrap—[sniffs]—currently and I typically galvanize my pipe in my furnace. But I’m ending up with molten leakage every damn where! [chuckles] So I’m lookin’ to harden my converter.
—Uh, what all do you have in your reserves up ‘ere? [exhales]
Well, I just came down from the cluster mill; that’s who gave me your phone number.
And they were sayin’ that they thought I was just needin’ ta refine my slag. So—
—do you think I should puddle my scrap or…? I’m kind of at a loss here, so… [sfx]—
—um, I’m not familiar with most of the terms you’re using.

—Okay? We’re just—we’re a service center. We sell raw steel products: shapes, like steel shapes, you know, that fabricate angle frames and square tubing and round tube and that kinda stuff; that’s what we do. We sell those kinda products—

—Okay! Well that sounds good! I don’t have access, myself, to a Sendzimer Mill. So I’ve been getting by with shot blasting or pickling, usually. So, could I use a stiffener or would it be too bulbous? Obviously, I don’t—
—Yeah, I don’t—yeah, I don’t—I don’t know your application. [speaking to co-worker] Ran—Randy? [to LPC ] Is that a—?
—Yeah. I’m sorry I can’t help you more today!—

—I was in the middle of busheling—No, it’s okay! I was just—uh, one other question, if I may—I was in the middle of busheling my clippings—[sniffs]—when my furnace started hot idling. And now the whole thing is just mothballing. Do you think you guys could, maybe, help me if I wanted to try scarfing the whole thing?
I—I wouldn’t be able to help you with any of that, no.
Oh! Uh, my dad, here, thinks that he could phrase it better. Would you talk to him? My dad Joe, here?
—Just here, he—let him know you talked to me; I’m Jim. Hold on a sec.
Fuck you! You’re gonna be in jail, motherchickenshit!
Hey! So did we get that worked out or…?
Okay. So, maybe if I took all my lum-bore [?] could we try slitting the width on that? Or would that hamper the ductility? At the fulcrum? [exhales] I—
—I am sorry, sir. I have no idea what you’re even talkin’ about.
I’m at a loss here! Big guy. That’s why I need your help—
—Yeah, I can’t help you! Yeah, I can’t help ya.
That’s fine. Thank you, now! Okay.
Hey, Joe. It’s Jimbo; I talked to ya earlier in the week. How ya doin’?

Hey good! Hey, I think I’ve hit upon a solution, uh, with all the crockery I’m forgin’ here. Like when I make a profiterole or a pudding or something, the bottom will always burn, ya know? [inhales] So—but I think if—if I had skelp, uh, to my bakeware it’ll be perfect! Then I could make jelly or meringue. Um, so maybe if I butt weld the skelp, what do you think on somethin’ like that? [sniffs] [exhales]
I have no idea what you’re talkin’ about, man!
Well, I—I’m ready to get castin’ with ya! I’m ready to pour here! Uh—
—Yeah, I don’t know what you’re talking about in any way, shape, or form!
—I don’t have—I don’t have anything going with you.

I’m a cook, okay? And I’m trying to get the right kinda crockery for all ma food! I’d like to put down a deposit. Maybe on some electrolytic tin plating or something; I think that would brighten my tubing. Oh, hey! That reminds me: do you guys ever go exotic? Like, ah, fabricate zirconium or somethin’ or do a hafnium extrusion or anything like that?
[sighs] ‘Cause I gotta have 48 canelé cooked by this weekend. And this Acapulco pot metal is just not cuttin’ it, uh, friend.
Yeah. Yeah, unfortunately, I’m not gonna be able to help ya out, buddy!
Well could I buy some nickel from ye? Just—
I’m gettin’ sinter in all my crusts here. And they crack. And I can’t be crackin’ crusts up here. [sighs]
I might have some welding rod out there, if you wanna come down ’n take a look at it.
—About all I would have—what I would—I would have for nickel.
Ye—maybe you could talk to my dad; he’s a metallurgist. May—he could explain it better, you know? Here, lemme get him on the line. Talk to Joe; his name’s Joe, also. Hey, Joe, talk to J—
Yeah? And why are you calling me?—
—I have—I have no idea no—I have no idea. You guys called me—
I have no idea what you guys need or talking—even talkin' about.
Why don’t you lay off, huh? Why don’t you lay off? Because I’m gonna beg God that you should drop dead if you interrogating me!
Don’t you believe in God? Why you keep on bothering me? You piece of Shit. Shit. Shit.
